title: Go SDK: streaming schema factory, retry annotations, and ergonomic typed decoders
preview:
Problem:
Teams building streaming LLM flows want typed, ergonomic decoding of structured outputs (JSON objects, jsonlines, and framed events) with clear semantics around retries. Current Go SDK helpers assume a single 'line-delimited JSON' framing and don't expose per-chunk retry safety signals. We need a model-agno...
text:
Problem:
Teams building streaming LLM flows want typed, ergonomic decoding of structured outputs (JSON objects, jsonlines, and framed events) with clear semantics around retries. Current Go SDK helpers assume a single 'line-delimited JSON' framing and don't expose per-chunk retry safety signals. We need a model-agnostic factory for stream decoders, a decoder registry keyed by model+route/schema, and a small retry-annotation protocol that server responses can include to indicate whether a chunk is retry-safe.

Goals:
- Provide a StreamDecoderFactory that returns Decoder[T] for any typed struct T to keep strong typing in app code.
- Support multiple framings: json-lines, ndjson, framed-events ("event:" prefix), and envelope-with-retry-hint.
- Add a lightweight RetryHint concept in the response envelope: server may set retry_hint=partial|safe|none per-chunk. Client SDK uses this to deter...

title: Priority wavefront admission and attention tiling
preview: Objective: Reduce 99th and 99.9th percentile end-to-end latency for mixed prefill+decode workloads by combining a priority 'wavefront' admission policy with an attention-tiling prefetch strategy and opportunistic kernel probing. Motivation: Current continuous batching handles average throughput well but exhibits lon...
text: Objective: Reduce 99th and 99.9th percentile end-to-end latency for mixed prefill+decode workloads by combining a priority 'wavefront' admission policy with an attention-tiling prefetch strategy and opportunistic kernel probing. Motivation: Current continuous batching handles average throughput well but exhibits long tails for heterogeneous request shapes and bursts that intermix long prefill requests with short chat turns. We need a low-risk runtime change that (1) improves tail latency without sacrificing throughput, (2) plays nicely with kernel selection heuristics, and (3) is rollable via canary. Key idea: when admitting prefill requests into a batch, tag each request with a wavefront priority derived from deadline, model shape, and observed per-tenant tail-profile. Admission then proceeds in a multi-pass wavefront: (a) fast-path wave for short/interactive requests (high priority)...

title: Continuous Profile Optimizer + Batch Guard
preview:
Problem: teams run with static batching settings that degrade cost or latency as traffic shapes change. We need a continuous, low-risk system that recommends batching and cache parameters per workload and can apply them safely with automatic rollback if regressions are detected.

This ticket scopes the first iterati...
text:
Problem: teams run with static batching settings that degrade cost or latency as traffic shapes change. We need a continuous, low-risk system that recommends batching and cache parameters per workload and can apply them safely with automatic rollback if regressions are detected.

This ticket scopes the first iteration: a service ('profile-optimizer') that synthesizes workload profiles from request telemetry, derives heuristic parameter candidates (batch_size_target, max_latency_budget_ms, kv_cache_prefix_ttl, coalescing_window_ms), validates candidates in a safety sandbox, and exposes a staged apply path (recommend -> canary -> apply) with rollback triggers based on production SLIs/QLIs.

title: Ledger anchoring for progressive streaming credits and adaptive throttles
preview: Problem: Streaming sessions that emit partial fragments (progressive outputs) are causing inconsistent token ledgers when combined with mid-stream model switches, dynamic throttles, and client reconnects. In several customer incidents we observed: (1) duplicate crediting when partial fragments are re-sent after reco...
text:
Problem: Streaming sessions that emit partial fragments (progressive outputs) are causing inconsistent token ledgers when combined with mid-stream model switches, dynamic throttles, and client reconnects. In several customer incidents we observed: (1) duplicate crediting when partial fragments are re-sent after reconnect, (2) under-crediting when preempted fragments are dropped by fallback routing, and (3) misattribution between input (prompt) and generated output tokens across multipart/noncanonical frames. This ticket implements a ledger anchoring mechanism plus an adaptive crediting protocol to make progressive streaming metering deterministic and robust to reconnects, backpressure, and model handoffs.

Scope: implement server-side ledger anchors per-session with idempotent fragment attribution; integrate anchors with the rate-limiter to ensure provisional credits can be rolled for...

title: Cross-Product Launch Calendar: Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator
preview: Objective: Build a Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator (DRTO) that provides a single authoritative launch calendar and automated conflict-resolution layer for runtime, console, and API releases. DRTO should allow teams to reserve launch slots, attach canary windows, declare blackout windows and capacity reservations,...
text:
Objective: Build a Dynamic Release Train Orchestrator (DRTO) that provides a single authoritative launch calendar and automated conflict-resolution layer for runtime, console, and API releases. DRTO should allow teams to reserve launch slots, attach canary windows, declare blackout windows and capacity reservations, and automatically compute safe stagger policies and fallback lanes when conflicts are detected.

Background: Launch friction has been a recurring source of latency and outages when multiple product teams schedule large runtime or console changes in overlapping windows. The current playbooks are manual (spreadsheets + Slack) and cause avoidable collisions, overbooked GPU capacity, and late-stage rollbacks. This initiative aims to reduce schedule conflicts, accelerate cross-team coordination, and provide automated signals for go/no-go decisions.

Scope (MVP):
- Central calen...

title: Strategic microcommit bands and rolling buffer allocation for feature waves
preview: Problem: As we scale Redwood Dedicated and Private onboarding, individual initiatives spike runway demand mid-cycle causing repeated mid-cycle scope cuts and last-minute reassignments. This ticket defines a lightweight, operational mechanism: 'microcommit bands' (small reserved effort bands per initiative) plus a ro...
text: Problem: As we scale Redwood Dedicated and Private onboarding, individual initiatives spike runway demand mid-cycle causing repeated mid-cycle scope cuts and last-minute reassignments. This ticket defines a lightweight, operational mechanism: 'microcommit bands' (small reserved effort bands per initiative) plus a rolling buffer allocation that moves between initiatives based on objective signals. Objective: reduce emergency scope cuts, increase predictability of cross-team staffing, and preserve high-priority launches without over-committing engineering capacity. Scope: define band sizing heuristic, buffer governance, automation hooks for signal ingestion (usage, blocker count, deploy risk), rollout plan for next three cycles, and dashboards to track buffer depletion and rebalancing activity.

title: Customer Advocate Handshake and Release Priority Protocol
preview: Problem: Major enterprise customers and strategic partners currently have ad-hoc escalation paths into the roadmap. That variability causes misaligned expectations, late-stage scope creep, and unclear commitments for both Product and Engineering. We lack a repeatable 'handshake' that turns a high-touch customer ask ...
text:
Problem: Major enterprise customers and strategic partners currently have ad-hoc escalation paths into the roadmap. That variability causes misaligned expectations, late-stage scope creep, and unclear commitments for both Product and Engineering. We lack a repeatable 'handshake' that turns a high-touch customer ask into a scoped, measurable roadmap commitment with clear fallbacks and billing/contract triggers.

Goal: Define and operationalize a lightweight but enforceable Customer Advocate Handshake (CAH) that: 1) standardizes intake for top accounts, 2) produces a scoped Linear ticket with acceptance criteria and rollout plan, 3) surfaces necessary legal/finance/ops triggers, and 4) maps the result to OKRs and release slots with clear canaries/fallbacks.

Proposed approach:
- Intake form filled by Customer Advocate (CA) including business impact, customer urgency, usage signals, expe...

title: Error Budget Federation and Cross-Team Commitment Matrix
preview:
Goal: Define and implement a federated error-budget model plus a commitment matrix so teams have predictable runway for feature rollouts while preserving global SLOs.

Problem: Currently error budgets are tracked only at a product-wide level. Team leads lack clear, actionable allocations and lightweight APIs to rese...
text:
Goal: Define and implement a federated error-budget model plus a commitment matrix so teams have predictable runway for feature rollouts while preserving global SLOs.

Problem: Currently error budgets are tracked only at a product-wide level. Team leads lack clear, actionable allocations and lightweight APIs to reserve tranches for experiments or rollouts. This leads to ad-hoc rollouts, surprise SLO burns, and emergency rollbacks.

Scope:
- Design allocation rules: baseline reservation for infra/critical services, per-product tranches, and experiment buckets.
- Implement a Commitment Matrix (spreadsheet + API) that maps teams -> weekly tranche -> guardrails.
- Extend SLO dashboard with per-team rollup and tranche-level burn charts.
- Integrate gating into Redwood Console: canary/rollout policies check team tranche before increasing % traffic.
- Alerts & runbook: automated alerts to ch...

title: Sprint Lab: Roadmap Threading & Scenario Lanes for Hosted API + Model Catalog
preview: Objective: Create a compact, decision-ready Q2 roadmap for Hosted API + Model Catalog that balances reliability SLAs, cost optimization, and an experimental runway for new open models. We will run a "Scenario Lab" over two sprints to validate trade-offs across latency, unit cost, and quality so leadership can commit...
text:
Objective: Create a compact, decision-ready Q2 roadmap for Hosted API + Model Catalog that balances reliability SLAs, cost optimization, and an experimental runway for new open models. We will run a "Scenario Lab" over two sprints to validate trade-offs across latency, unit cost, and quality so leadership can commit tranche dates and capacity hedges.

Why now: Usage growth and new open-model variants require a clearer phasing plan to avoid surprise capacity costs and quality regressions. Current workstreams (model onboarding, runtime kernel selection, and console rollout) are loosely aligned; this ticket unifies them into lane-based deliverables and explicit OKR ties.

High-level approach: split the roadmap into three scenario lanes: (1) Stability Lane — SLA-backed endpoints and conservative model rotations; (2) Cost Lane — quantized/packed variants and batching strategies for cost-se...

title: Sprint orchestra and fallback-lane runbook for heterogeneous runtime rollouts
preview:
Overview:
This ticket captures a runbook, ownership matrix, and sprint-level cadence for rolling out runtime changes (kernel swaps, batching logic, and quantization toggles) across heterogeneous fleet segments (hosted, dedicated, private). Goal is predictable latency SLOs while enabling fast roll-forward for critica...
text:
Overview:
This ticket captures a runbook, ownership matrix, and sprint-level cadence for rolling out runtime changes (kernel swaps, batching logic, and quantization toggles) across heterogeneous fleet segments (hosted, dedicated, private). Goal is predictable latency SLOs while enabling fast roll-forward for critical fixes.

Scope & Acceptance Criteria:
- Define orchestration lanes (canary, regional wave, enterprise-only) with clear capacity hedges and warmup windows.
- Document fallback lanes and automated routing (policy) to downgrade to compatible model variants or older runtime build.
- Provide a checklist for preflight verifications: perf bench (p50/p95), KV cache hit validation, tracing hooks.
- Create post-deploy telemetry dashboard and regression alert thresholds.

Progress updates:
- 2026-02-22: Draft runbook created (Confluence: https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/RRO/...

title: probe failure-mode: prevent cascading capacity churn during region failover
preview: Problem: During a recent multi-region failover exercise we observed cascading capacity churn and transient 5xxs as readiness probes oscillated for pods coming up in the target region. Rapid pod restarts and immediate autoscaler triggers amplified the problem: probes flapped, the HPA scaled up aggressively, new pods ...
text:
Problem: During a recent multi-region failover exercise we observed cascading capacity churn and transient 5xxs as readiness probes oscillated for pods coming up in the target region. Rapid pod restarts and immediate autoscaler triggers amplified the problem: probes flapped, the HPA scaled up aggressively, new pods hit cold KV cache misses, latency SLOs breached, and circuit breakers opened downstream.

Goal: Implement a set of defensive changes to probe behavior, autoscaler stabilization, and load-shedding coordination so that region failovers and partial network/dep degradations do not cause secondary outages or SLO regressions. We want measurable improvements in availability and reduced variability during failover while keeping recovery time acceptable.

title: operator fencing caused cascading tenant reconcile delays; staged hotfix and staggered redeploy plan
preview: Summary: Multiple on-prem customers reported prolonged tenant upgrade stalls after control-plane operator fencing events. Symptoms: operator enters short-lived fence state, leader election flips rapidly, tenant controllers delay reconciles for 10+ minutes leading to customer application errors and upgrade timeouts. ...
text: Summary: Multiple on-prem customers reported prolonged tenant upgrade stalls after control-plane operator fencing events. Symptoms: operator enters short-lived fence state, leader election flips rapidly, tenant controllers delay reconciles for 10+ minutes leading to customer application errors and upgrade timeouts. Impacted modes: Private (VPC) and on-prem deployments using helm-managed operator. This ticket tracks triage, an emergency hotfix branch, rollout plan, and post-deploy verification.

title: Per-route & model slice rollup summarizer with federated rollups
preview: Design and implement a resilient summarization service that computes per-route / per-model / per-customer micro-rollups for cost and latency. The service should produce compact, queryable artifacts (hourly and 5m rollups) usable by Console dashboards, billing exports, and SLO evaluators. The focus is on federated, t...
text: Design and implement a resilient summarization service that computes per-route / per-model / per-customer micro-rollups for cost and latency. The service should produce compact, queryable artifacts (hourly and 5m rollups) usable by Console dashboards, billing exports, and SLO evaluators. The focus is on federated, tenant-aware aggregation to minimize tail-cardinality and support streaming-friendly increments and efficient backfills.

title: Staged ledger consolidation with probabilistic idempotency and windowed settlement
preview: Problem: We currently observe intermittent divergence between real-time meter tallies and final invoice-ready ledgers when late events (retries, region duplicates) arrive after the settlement window. Root causes include non-deterministic batching, out-of-order partition delivery, and opportunistic dedupe heuristics ...
text:
Problem: We currently observe intermittent divergence between real-time meter tallies and final invoice-ready ledgers when late events (retries, region duplicates) arrive after the settlement window. Root causes include non-deterministic batching, out-of-order partition delivery, and opportunistic dedupe heuristics that are conservative and sometimes discard valid retroactive corrections.

Goal: Build a staged consolidation pipeline that performs windowed settlement with an explicit probabilistic idempotency scoring pass followed by a deterministic commit phase. The pipeline should: (1) accept late-arriving events with bounded correction windows, (2) provide deterministic final ledgers for billing, (3) support shadow-run canaries for safe rollout, and (4) surface lineage for audit and support.

High-level design:
- Ingest: events enter via existing usage-stream (Kafka topic usage.v2)....

title: Quantization profiles: mixed‑precision throughput vs quality contour benchmarking
preview: Goal: Build a benchmark and load harness that maps throughput (tokens/sec) and p95 latency to quality for a range of quantization profiles (FP16, INT8, INT4, and mixed-precision fallbacks). This ticket covers spec, harness implementation, quality metric plumbing, regression detection rules, and dashboards to visuali...
text:
Goal: Build a benchmark and load harness that maps throughput (tokens/sec) and p95 latency to quality for a range of quantization profiles (FP16, INT8, INT4, and mixed-precision fallbacks). This ticket covers spec, harness implementation, quality metric plumbing, regression detection rules, and dashboards to visualize quality/perf contours and automated alerts when a quant profile crosses a regression threshold.

Scope and constraints:
- Run mixed-precision permutations where compute kernels use FP16 for attention and INT8/INT4 for MLP where supported. Include explicit fallback variants (e.g., INT4->INT8->FP16) and measure cost/latency tradeoffs.
- Collect tokens/sec, p50/p95/p99 latency, memory footprint, kernel utilization, and end-to-end quality metrics (token-level perplexity proxy + embedding cosine similarity and ROUGE on a canonical prompt set).
- Support traffic modes: synthet...

title: Cross‑lang ops starter pack: pragmatic streaming & retries templates
preview:
Goal: Ship a compact, opinionated starter pack (repo + docs) that shows pragmatic patterns for streaming ergonomics, deterministic retries, and small server/CLI examples across TypeScript (Next.js), Python (FastAPI), and Go (HTTP server + CLI).

Why: We keep getting support tickets and onboarding questions around ho...
text:
Goal: Ship a compact, opinionated starter pack (repo + docs) that shows pragmatic patterns for streaming ergonomics, deterministic retries, and small server/CLI examples across TypeScript (Next.js), Python (FastAPI), and Go (HTTP server + CLI).

Why: We keep getting support tickets and onboarding questions around how to wire streaming responses into modern frontends and servers while handling retries, cancellation, and partial result resumption. This ticket bundles lightweight, production-minded samples and cookbook snippets teams can copy into their apps.

Deliverables:
- nextjs-edge-streaming: Minimal Next.js app showing client-side streaming consumption (fetch with ReadableStream + AbortController), token-level progress UI, and a server-edge route that proxies Redwood streaming responses with correct headers.
- fastapi-async-stream: FastAPI sample demonstrating an async generator f...

title: Automated VPC Remediator + Runbook for Common Private Healthcheck Failures (Terraform-aware)
preview: Goal: Ship a defensible, safe-to-run automated remediator plus an on-call runbook that addresses the most frequent Private (VPC) healthcheck failures. This ticket covers: (1) canonical detection thresholds and alert mappings; (2) an automation state machine that performs idempotent corrective actions; (3) tight Terr...
text: Goal: Ship a defensible, safe-to-run automated remediator plus an on-call runbook that addresses the most frequent Private (VPC) healthcheck failures. This ticket covers: (1) canonical detection thresholds and alert mappings; (2) an automation state machine that performs idempotent corrective actions; (3) tight Terraform/IaC repair hooks to fix drift without risking destructive changes; (4) canary and rollout gating for upgrades that touch private nodes; and (5) clear rollback and escalation paths for oncall.

title: Sinuous regional routing policy: composite latency + capacity steering with safe fallbacks
preview: Design and implement a new multi-region request steering policy (codename: 'sinuous') that combines latency-based routing, geo pinning for affinity, and capacity-aware steering into a single compact decision function. The goal is to improve SLO adherence for 99th pct latency during cross-region incidents while minim...
text: Design and implement a new multi-region request steering policy (codename: 'sinuous') that combines latency-based routing, geo pinning for affinity, and capacity-aware steering into a single compact decision function. The goal is to improve SLO adherence for 99th pct latency during cross-region incidents while minimizing unnecessary cross-region egress and avoiding capacity hot-spots. The policy must integrate circuit breaker signals and graded load-shedding tiers for graceful degradation.

title: Outlier-resistant quantile summarizer for inference KPIs
preview: Define and ship a memory-efficient, outlier-resistant streaming quantile summarizer to produce stable p50/p90/p99 estimates for per-tenant LLM inference KPIs across heterogeneous ingestion paths (OTel histograms, Prometheus buckets, and custom adaptor events). This component will live in the telemetry ingestion laye...
text: Define and ship a memory-efficient, outlier-resistant streaming quantile summarizer to produce stable p50/p90/p99 estimates for per-tenant LLM inference KPIs across heterogeneous ingestion paths (OTel histograms, Prometheus buckets, and custom adaptor events). This component will live in the telemetry ingestion layer and provide both per-tenant and global summaries with explicit handling for bursty edge nodes and noisy tails.

title: Transient Limiter Outage Recovery Agent - SLO-preserving soft-admission and cross-region reconciliation
preview: Background: Recent incidents show that transient outages of the global limiter control plane can cause either hard denials (customers blocked) or uncontrolled open-admit behavior during failover windows, both of which cause SLO violations. Problem statement: we need a mechanism to bridge short-lived limiter outages ...
text: Background: Recent incidents show that transient outages of the global limiter control plane can cause either hard denials (customers blocked) or uncontrolled open-admit behavior during failover windows, both of which cause SLO violations. Problem statement: we need a mechanism to bridge short-lived limiter outages without violating latency and error SLOs and while preserving per-tenant fairness and billing correctness. Goal: design and implement a locally-run Recovery Agent that proxies admission decisions when the primary quota store is unavailable. The Agent must be SLO-aware (maintain p50/p95/p99 latency budgets), tenant-aware (avoid starvation), and reconciliatory (merge local state back to global quota store after recovery). Core idea: a soft-admission proxy with three coupled capabilities: 1) Latency-aware admission: per-tenant residual latency budget tokens determine how aggre...

title: Topology-aware KV-tiling continuous batcher for mixed-length streams
preview: Background: Continuous batching performs well for homogeneous sequence lengths but we see persistent GPU memory fragmentation and tail latency increases when request mixes contain many short prompts interleaved with long responses. Current KV residency and slab allocation are conservative and cause underutilized GPU...
text:
Background: Continuous batching performs well for homogeneous sequence lengths but we see persistent GPU memory fragmentation and tail latency increases when request mixes contain many short prompts interleaved with long responses. Current KV residency and slab allocation are conservative and cause underutilized GPU memory and frequent kv-evictions during long-turn spikes.

Scope: Implement a topology-aware KV-tiling policy in the continuous batcher that (a) partitions KV residency into per-cohort tiles by length/turn profile, (b) uses a lightweight reservoir admission controller to pin tiles temporarily during long-response phases, and (c) coordinates with the slot allocator to bias packing so that mixed-length cohorts co-reside in a cache-friendly layout. The change must preserve latency SLOs for P0 routes and reduce average token cost by lowering unnecessary evictions and GPU slab ...

title: Release-train comms, ops runbook, and release-notes digest for API/Console/Runtime
preview: Summary: Coordinate a single, consolidated release-train communications and operational runbook that covers milestones, scope-lock signals, cut criteria, release-notes generation, and GTM tasks across API, Console, and Runtime teams. Goal is to reduce ambiguity about last-minute scope decisions, automate a digest fo...
text:
Summary: Coordinate a single, consolidated release-train communications and operational runbook that covers milestones, scope-lock signals, cut criteria, release-notes generation, and GTM tasks across API, Console, and Runtime teams. Goal is to reduce ambiguity about last-minute scope decisions, automate a digest for release notes, and codify a lightweight hypercare plan for the first 72 hours post-rollout.

Background: Recent rollouts had fragmented comms between runtime and console teams resulting in mismatched docs, late bugfix rollouts, and duplicated rollback efforts. This initiative centralizes cutline decisions and creates an automated release-notes digest that compiles: PRs merged after freeze, critical known issues, upgrade notes, and per-team owner blurbs.

Scope:
- Milestone calendar and signoff checklist for MAJOR, MINOR, and PATCH release lanes.
- Cut criteria matrix (fun...

title: Dedicated: per-tenant/cluster batching tier overrides + precedence rules
preview:
Dedicated customers need deterministic control of batching tier/profile selection with clear override precedence.

Implement:
- Per-tenant override: ability for Dedicated tenants to force a batching tier (fast|standard|cost_efficient) and/or explicit batching knob overrides (where allowed) at the tenant/cluster leve...
text:
Dedicated customers need deterministic control of batching tier/profile selection with clear override precedence.

Implement:
- Per-tenant override: ability for Dedicated tenants to force a batching tier (fast|standard|cost_efficient) and/or explicit batching knob overrides (where allowed) at the tenant/cluster level.
- Cluster override: ability for Redwood ops to pin a cluster-level default tier for a Dedicated cluster when capacity characteristics differ (GPU type mix, MIG, etc.).
- Precedence definition (must be documented and enforced consistently across control plane + runtime):
 1) Customer override (tenant/cluster level in Dedicated) 
 2) Route policy tier selection (route/model policy configuration)
 3) Tier default selected by workload-aware logic (runtime heuristics)

Non-goals (for this ticket):
- Per-request overrides for Dedicated (we may add later; risk of abuse/high car...

title: Implement tiered batching profile config schema + validation (incl. migration)
preview:
Goal
Ship a backwards-compatible config schema for three batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient), plus validation rules and a migration plan for existing manual batching configs.

Context
We’re introducing tiered defaults that map to scheduler knobs. The runtime needs a clear, stable configuration contract...
text:
Goal
Ship a backwards-compatible config schema for three batching SLO tiers (fast/standard/cost-efficient), plus validation rules and a migration plan for existing manual batching configs.

Context
We’re introducing tiered defaults that map to scheduler knobs. The runtime needs a clear, stable configuration contract that:
- works across Hosted API, Dedicated, and Private deployments
- supports route-level policy selection and per-tenant overrides (Dedicated)
- prevents foot-guns (conflicting knobs, impossible combinations, accidental global tier changes)
- supports safe rollout (feature flag, kill switch, and easy rollback)

Scope (this ticket)
1) Define the config schema (control plane + runtime consumption)
2) Implement validation + precedence rules
3) Provide migration behavior for existing configs
4) Add docs/notes for downstream integrators (Console + SDKs) on the schema contract...

title: Harden marketplace entitlement sync job (retries, idempotency, alerting)
preview:
Context
Marketplace buyers (AWS + GCP) are hitting provisioning delays after subscribing because our entitlement sync is occasionally failing silently, retrying too slowly, or producing inconsistent results across repeated runs. This directly impacts the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning funnel and has b...
text:
Context
Marketplace buyers (AWS + GCP) are hitting provisioning delays after subscribing because our entitlement sync is occasionally failing silently, retrying too slowly, or producing inconsistent results across repeated runs. This directly impacts the subscription → entitlement → org provisioning funnel and has been a recurring escalation path (see SUP-4821).

Goal
Improve reliability and debuggability of the entitlement sync job:
- Faster and safer recovery from transient partner/API issues (retries + backoff)
- Idempotency so repeated sync runs do not create duplicate entitlements / churn state
- Strong alerting and runbook to reduce time-to-detect/resolve

Scope
Applies to:
- Scheduled entitlement sync job (poll + reconcile)
- On-demand sync trigger used by console onboarding retry flows
- AWS Marketplace Entitlement / Metering integration and GCP Marketplace procurement entitle...

title: Oscillating prefetch orchestrator for mixed-length continuous scheduler tuning
preview: Problem: Mixed prompt/response workloads (web chat + API summarization) cause the continuous-batcher to oscillate between aggressive prefetching and conservative slot allocation. That oscillation increases GPU memory churn and KV cache fragmentation, resulting in 10-25% tail latency spikes and up to 12% higher memor...
text:
Problem: Mixed prompt/response workloads (web chat + API summarization) cause the continuous-batcher to oscillate between aggressive prefetching and conservative slot allocation. That oscillation increases GPU memory churn and KV cache fragmentation, resulting in 10-25% tail latency spikes and up to 12% higher memory pressure on p4d nodes in production.

Goal: Reduce oscillation and stabilize KV residency while preserving user-facing latency SLOs for short-turn interactive flows and long-turn generation. Implement a scheduler-level damping mechanism ("oscillation damping") and a KV bank rebalancer that proactively coils prefetched KV into compact banks to reduce fragmentation during bursts. Validate on stage with synthetic mixed-length traces and a small customer synthetic traffic test.

Context: We've tuned length-aware batching and prefetch windows independently, but the coupling be...

title: Marketplace metering dimension mapping (AWS/GCP) aligned to SKU catalog v2
preview:
## Context
Marketplace refresh introduces new SKUs (Hosted / Dedicated / Private + add-ons). Engineering needs to ensure that:
1) Marketplace billing dimensions (AWS + GCP) match the SKU catalog v2 naming/IDs
2) The internal billing pipeline can unambiguously map marketplace usage records to Redwood meters and invoi...
text:
## Context
Marketplace refresh introduces new SKUs (Hosted / Dedicated / Private + add-ons). Engineering needs to ensure that:
1) Marketplace billing dimensions (AWS + GCP) match the SKU catalog v2 naming/IDs
2) The internal billing pipeline can unambiguously map marketplace usage records to Redwood meters and invoice line items
3) Entitlements and limits derived from the subscription resolve to the correct plan/SKU in Console onboarding

We have existing legacy marketplace dimensions and internal meters that do not align 1:1 with the new catalog, and we already saw a mismatch in the wild (ref: SUP-4829 + Slack #eng thread “billing dimension mismatch found”). This is a launch blocker because it affects customer trust, invoice reconciliation, and marketplace review.

## Problem statement
Today, marketplace usage events and invoice lines can be inconsistent due to:
- Dimension name drif...

title: Marketplace entitlement verification API (AWS/GCP) for subscription → org provisioning flow
preview:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

T...
text:
## Context
Marketplace buyers currently hit a confusing failure mode in the first 30–90 minutes after subscription: Redwood Console can’t reliably determine whether the customer is entitled (and to what SKU limits), especially when entitlement propagation is delayed or when the customer already has a Redwood org.

This ticket implements/extends control-plane API endpoints used by the marketplace onboarding entrypoint to:
1) verify a buyer’s AWS/GCP marketplace subscription is active and mapped to a Redwood SKU
2) return a stable entitlement object (or a clear, retryable error) that Console + provisioning can act on
3) support edge cases (existing org linking, multiple subscriptions, delayed propagation)

This is a critical dependency for the marketplace refresh launch (time-to-first-token + reduced support escalations).

## Goals / Non-goals
### Goals
- Provide a single verification A...

title: Segment pruning + materialized views to accelerate tenant dashboard reads
preview: Background: Interactive tenant dashboards (Usage Overview, Top Models, Live Tokens) currently suffer from high p95 query latencies during peak windows. Queries that scan timeseries records for the last 30/90/365 days frequently hit deep partitions and large indices, producing median p95s of ~850ms and spikes >2.5s f...
text:
Background: Interactive tenant dashboards (Usage Overview, Top Models, Live Tokens) currently suffer from high p95 query latencies during peak windows. Queries that scan timeseries records for the last 30/90/365 days frequently hit deep partitions and large indices, producing median p95s of ~850ms and spikes >2.5s for multi-tenant orgs. The usage analytics pipeline emits fine-grained events (per-request tokens, model_id, tenant_id, region) which are stored in the analytics store and aggregated at read time for the console dashboards.

Problem statement: Many dashboard queries are read-heavy, low-cardinality (top-K, timeseries rolls) and can be answered from pre-aggregated state. Current query plan often does full index range scans for scenes where we could apply segment (time) pruning and use compact MV (daily/hourly) to avoid scanning hot partitions. Deep offset pagination exacerbate...

title: Quant QA harness: golden-corpus orchestration, stratified sampling, and semantic gating
preview: Goal: Build an automated Quantization QA harness that validates FP8/INT8 calibration profiles against a verifiable golden corpus and produces deterministic pass/fail gates suitable for CI, canary rollouts, and Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket focuses on the orchestration layer and validation logic: golden ...
text:
Goal: Build an automated Quantization QA harness that validates FP8/INT8 calibration profiles against a verifiable golden corpus and produces deterministic pass/fail gates suitable for CI, canary rollouts, and Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket focuses on the orchestration layer and validation logic: golden corpus selection and curation, stratified sampling, semantic + token-level metrics, composite gating rules, artifacts storage, and CI integration.

Why: Current quantization validation runs produce ad-hoc reports and manual signoffs. We need reproducible, auditable, and explainable gates so model owners can safely accept quant profiles into runtime and autoscale pools without regressing customer-facing quality.

Scope:
- Define golden-corpus curation process and metadata schema (source, intent, distribution buckets, provenance).
- Implement stratified sampler that ensures c...

title: Declarative onboarding bundler, publish pipeline and canary retraction workflow
preview: Build a declarative CI/CD flow that takes a model bundle (model artifact + manifest + perf-profile), performs automated signing and provenance capture, publishes a staged performance profile, and manages a canary rollout with automated retraction/rollback when regressions are detected. This ticket focuses on the orc...
text: Build a declarative CI/CD flow that takes a model bundle (model artifact + manifest + perf-profile), performs automated signing and provenance capture, publishes a staged performance profile, and manages a canary rollout with automated retraction/rollback when regressions are detected. This ticket focuses on the orchestration glue (bundler + publisher + retraction policy) and does not change runtime kernel code.

title: Chronicle Atlas: Micro-journey Mapping & Cost Lensing Prototype
preview: Goal: Prototype a compact, context-aware navigation overlay (Chronicle Atlas) that surfaces micro-journeys across Console surfaces (Dashboards, Rollouts, Cost Views) and folds in a lightweight cost-lensing control. The Atlas is intended to reduce cognitive load when switching contexts, surface relevant rollouts and ...
text: Goal: Prototype a compact, context-aware navigation overlay (Chronicle Atlas) that surfaces micro-journeys across Console surfaces (Dashboards, Rollouts, Cost Views) and folds in a lightweight cost-lensing control. The Atlas is intended to reduce cognitive load when switching contexts, surface relevant rollouts and cost metrics inline, and provide predictable routing behavior for multi-workspace customers. Scope: IA sketch -> mid-fidelity prototype -> internal usability session -> iterate on placement/interaction. Out of scope for this ticket: backend telemetry plumbing, RBAC enforcement, and Dedicated deployment config view changes (these are follow-ups tracked under ENG tickets).

title: Adaptive-window token valuation & microbilling bridge for streaming requests
preview: Problem: Our current billing pipeline attributes token costs using ingestion-time windows and naive dedupe that breaks for long-lived streaming sessions and multi-part chunked responses. Late-arriving events (network retries, out-of-order shards) cause either double-billing or missing charges, and tenants with high ...
text: Problem: Our current billing pipeline attributes token costs using ingestion-time windows and naive dedupe that breaks for long-lived streaming sessions and multi-part chunked responses. Late-arriving events (network retries, out-of-order shards) cause either double-billing or missing charges, and tenants with high variability (low-latency mobile clients) see noisy spikes. Goal: Build a streaming-aware microbilling bridge that performs event-time aligned token valuation with adaptive watermarking, deterministic request fingerprints for idempotent dedupe, and a tenant-configurable grace window. The bridge should operate as a lightweight pre-aggregation layer between the streaming ingestion gateway and the existing ledger reconciler so we can deploy it incrementally without changing downstream billing semantics.

title: Geo provisioning orchestration spec and sales handback guide
preview: Objective: Define a pragmatic orchestration specification and a repeatable sales handback guide for enterprise region/data-residency requests (new region provisioning, failover enablement, and strict residency constraints). This doc ties eligibility checks, cost estimation, provisioning runway, expected lead times, ...
text:
Objective: Define a pragmatic orchestration specification and a repeatable sales handback guide for enterprise region/data-residency requests (new region provisioning, failover enablement, and strict residency constraints). This doc ties eligibility checks, cost estimation, provisioning runway, expected lead times, and a clear handoff checklist for Sales/SEs into a single pipeline that can be operationalized by PM, Solutions, and Infra teams.

Context: Customers increasingly ask for region-specific availability for compliance, latency, and contractual residency. Current intake is ad-hoc: Sales submits email threads, Infra does ad-hoc sizing, and PMs coordinate case-by-case. That creates long lead times and inconsistent contractual language. We need a spec that: 1) standardizes decision gates (eligibility, risk, capacity), 2) produces a fast, defensible cost and timeline estimate, and ...

title: SLO-horizon interruptor with KV tenancy decay and progressive preemption
preview: Problem: Continuous batching improves GPU utilization but introduces complex preemption trade-offs when GPU memory (KV cache + model state) becomes constrained. Current hard-preempt approaches either violate tenant latency SLOs or evict cache too aggressively, causing quality regressions and thundering re-warm. Goal...
text: Problem: Continuous batching improves GPU utilization but introduces complex preemption trade-offs when GPU memory (KV cache + model state) becomes constrained. Current hard-preempt approaches either violate tenant latency SLOs or evict cache too aggressively, causing quality regressions and thundering re-warm. Goal: design and implement an SLO-aware interruptor that (1) minimizes SLO breaches, (2) reduces harmful KV thrash, and (3) reclaims memory in a graded, predictable way.

title: API Token Retirement, Escrow & Scope Assertion Playbook
preview: Problem: Current token lifecycle is inconsistent across hosted/Dedicated/Private. We lack a single playbook for: emergency revocation, escrowed rotation, deterministic naming, and cryptographic scope assertions that tie tokens to RBAC v2 principals. This causes delayed responses to incidents and makes forensic audit...
text:
Problem: Current token lifecycle is inconsistent across hosted/Dedicated/Private. We lack a single playbook for: emergency revocation, escrowed rotation, deterministic naming, and cryptographic scope assertions that tie tokens to RBAC v2 principals. This causes delayed responses to incidents and makes forensic audit trails noisy.

Goal: Produce an operational playbook and implement infra pieces to enable: (1) automated rotation with escrow and phased retirement, (2) per-scope token issuance (least privilege by default), (3) deterministic, searchable naming conventions, (4) instant cross-region revocation propagation, and (5) explicit audit links between token events and SSO/SAML/Identity assertions.

Scope: Hosted API tokens, service-account tokens, per-scope scoped keys (read/write/embed), admin tokens used by Console and internal services. Not in scope: customer-managed HSM key mate...

title: Tenant-aware Tempo ingest adapter with ordered ACKs and dead-letter handling
preview: Build a Tempo-compatible ingest adapter that sits in front of our Tempo/OTLP export path to handle legacy batched span envelopes (Zipkin/Jaeger HTTP batches, older OTLP variants). The adapter must: (1) accept multiple legacy formats and normalize to OTLP v1; (2) perform per-tenant rate adaptation and backpressure to...
text: Build a Tempo-compatible ingest adapter that sits in front of our Tempo/OTLP export path to handle legacy batched span envelopes (Zipkin/Jaeger HTTP batches, older OTLP variants). The adapter must: (1) accept multiple legacy formats and normalize to OTLP v1; (2) perform per-tenant rate adaptation and backpressure to prevent noisy tenants from impacting others; (3) provide ordered ACK semantics for fanout scenarios so that downstream replays/dual-writes maintain causal ordering where required; (4) surface malformed or unverifiable batches to a dead-letter store with metadata for debugging; and (5) expose tracing and SLO metrics for ingestion latency, queue depth, dropped/bounced batches, and dead-letter rates. This component will be deployed as a regional, horizontally-scalable service alongside the existing OTLP ingress gateway and will be the primary compatibility shim during a phase...

title: Control plane: extend route config with quantization_profile_ref (default-safe) + guardrails
preview:
## Context
We are adding an opt-in Quantization Profile Library. For v1, enablement is route-level (not tenant-wide default), and default behavior must be unchanged.

This ticket extends the control-plane route configuration schema to include a quantization profile reference (`quantization_profile_ref`) that can be:...
text:
## Context
We are adding an opt-in Quantization Profile Library. For v1, enablement is route-level (not tenant-wide default), and default behavior must be unchanged.

This ticket extends the control-plane route configuration schema to include a quantization profile reference (`quantization_profile_ref`) that can be:
- `default` (or unset): preserve baseline precision behavior
- an explicit profile ref: apply a specific published profile version

This work also includes guardrails and migration notes so that existing routes remain valid, and incompatible/unknown values fail safely.

## Goals
1) Add a route-level config field for quantization enablement that is backwards compatible.
2) Ensure safe behavior when the field is missing/invalid/incompatible (do not break serving; fall back to baseline).
3) Provide clear API semantics + error messaging for Console and SDK consumers.
4) Land a...

title: Endpoint locality declaration wizard and attestation bundle export
preview: Add a guided 'locality declaration' experience when customers create or update a hosted endpoint, plus a backend job that composes a signed attestation bundle (location assertions, retention policy, TTLs, and routing traces) and exposes an export endpoint for audit ingestion. This ticket covers product spec, accepta...
text: Add a guided 'locality declaration' experience when customers create or update a hosted endpoint, plus a backend job that composes a signed attestation bundle (location assertions, retention policy, TTLs, and routing traces) and exposes an export endpoint for audit ingestion. This ticket covers product spec, acceptance criteria, API contract, and rollout plan for a canary release.

title: Policy engine support for stage gates (deterministic eval, caching, failure semantics)
preview:
## Context
Dedicated staged rollouts introduce explicit rollout stages (e.g., 1%/10%/50%/100% traffic or replica partitions) for Dedicated pools. Promotion between stages must be gated on objective signals (error rate, p95/p99 latency, saturation/headroom, GPU health, OOM, drops/429, etc.).

This ticket adds *first-...
text:
## Context
Dedicated staged rollouts introduce explicit rollout stages (e.g., 1%/10%/50%/100% traffic or replica partitions) for Dedicated pools. Promotion between stages must be gated on objective signals (error rate, p95/p99 latency, saturation/headroom, GPU health, OOM, drops/429, etc.).

This ticket adds *first-class gate evaluation* to the policy engine so that:
- Gate results are deterministic and auditable.
- The system behaves predictably under telemetry lag/outages (failure handling semantics are explicit).
- Evaluation is cheap enough to run continuously without impacting request routing latency.

Non-goal: building a general experimentation system. Gates are used strictly to decide whether a rollout stage is eligible for promotion / should auto-abort.

## Requirements
### Functional
1) Implement gate evaluation for DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan stages in the policy engine.
 - In...
tags: Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools

title: Precision-aware attention microkernel arbiter with speculative zero-copy demotion
preview:
Problem:
Serving runtime currently pays a measurable per-token cost on decode when switching between high-precision (FP16/FP32) and low-precision (INT8/FP8) attention kernels due to dequantization/ requantization, shadow KV copy semantics, and kernel setup overhead. These costs are most visible in workloads with mix...
text:
Problem:
Serving runtime currently pays a measurable per-token cost on decode when switching between high-precision (FP16/FP32) and low-precision (INT8/FP8) attention kernels due to dequantization/ requantization, shadow KV copy semantics, and kernel setup overhead. These costs are most visible in workloads with mixed sequence length distributions and high prefix reuse (prefill-heavy chat workloads) where some heads or layers benefit from reduced precision but others do not.

Goal:
Design and implement an attention microkernel arbiter that chooses the optimal precision execution path at microkernel granularity, and that can speculatively apply a "zero-copy demotion" window to avoid immediate dequant/requant when short-lived precision switches are expected. The arbiter should: 1) minimize end-to-end latency regression for single-request decode paths, 2) reduce token-level cost by favor...

title: Screenshot style matrix and redaction strategies for onboarding assets
preview: Goal: Create a single, prescriptive style matrix and automated redaction strategy for onboarding/quickstart screenshots and doc visuals that guarantees brand consistency, protects PII, and is safe across locales and platform sizes. Deliver a matrix of canonical compositions (compact, regular, extended), locale-aware...
text:
Goal: Create a single, prescriptive style matrix and automated redaction strategy for onboarding/quickstart screenshots and doc visuals that guarantees brand consistency, protects PII, and is safe across locales and platform sizes. Deliver a matrix of canonical compositions (compact, regular, extended), locale-aware crop rules, rule-based redaction masks, and an observable QA checklist so PM/Docs/Support can reliably produce release-ready assets without manual rework.

Scope:
- Define canonical screenshot compositions for three densities and two aspect ratios (desktop, tablet).
- Redaction strategies: token replacement (faux data), patterned blur, and edge-preserving mask overlays; map each rule to sensitivity level (public, internal, sensitive).
- Localization safety: anchoring rules for LTR/RTL languages; overflow handling for long translations; typographic fallbacks.
- Branding ove...

title: Stable-channel image mirroring and artifact contract for CUDA/driver stacks
preview: Establish a stable-channel image mirroring workflow and a machine-readable artifact contract for CUDA and driver stacks to guarantee predictable upgrades across Dedicated and Private deployments. The aim is to enable a single trusted 'stable' registry mirror per region that contains signed images, SBOMs, and a compa...
text: Establish a stable-channel image mirroring workflow and a machine-readable artifact contract for CUDA and driver stacks to guarantee predictable upgrades across Dedicated and Private deployments. The aim is to enable a single trusted 'stable' registry mirror per region that contains signed images, SBOMs, and a compatibility contract describing which driver/CUDA/kernel ABI combinations are validated for each runtime image.

title: Cross-customer k-fold relevance harness & live NDCG/MRR signal plumbing
preview: Overview: Build a reusable k-fold evaluation harness and live telemetry plumbing to track NDCG and MRR per-customer and per-promptset, enable stratified sampling for offline evals, and add lightweight A/B hooks on the reranking endpoint for experiment gating. This ticket covers the eval harness, schema changes for s...
text: Overview: Build a reusable k-fold evaluation harness and live telemetry plumbing to track NDCG and MRR per-customer and per-promptset, enable stratified sampling for offline evals, and add lightweight A/B hooks on the reranking endpoint for experiment gating. This ticket covers the eval harness, schema changes for signal ingestion, a reference Jupyter/Notion notebook for analysts, and a plan for a gradual rollout with canary A/B tests. Goals: 1) Produce consistent offline NDCG/MRR scores across customer prompt sets and model variants; 2) Surface live NDCG/MRR baselines in the Console and tie regression alerts to prompt sets; 3) Ensure reranker endpoint changes do not degrade P95 latency beyond 10ms and scale to 500 QPS with batching; 4) Provide simple experiment flags for product teams to run canary comparisons with minimal infra changes.

title: SDK artifact attestation and cross-registry publish flow (PyPI / npm / Go modules)
preview: Implement an end-to-end artifact attestation and publish flow for Redwood SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go). Goals: 1) produce deterministic, provenance-bearing artifacts (wheels/tarballs, npm packages, go modules), 2) sign and attest artifacts using Sigstore + in-toto where possible, 3) extend CI to perform reproducibl...
text: Implement an end-to-end artifact attestation and publish flow for Redwood SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Go). Goals: 1) produce deterministic, provenance-bearing artifacts (wheels/tarballs, npm packages, go modules), 2) sign and attest artifacts using Sigstore + in-toto where possible, 3) extend CI to perform reproducible builds and cross-registry publishes, 4) add lightweight runtime verification helpers in each SDK so enterprise customers can validate signatures locally. This ticket covers CI pipelines, release matrix changes, registry credentials handling, and adding examples demonstrating verification. Coordination needed with SDK maintainers to ensure packaging changes do not alter runtime behavior (esp. streaming ergonomics and retry wrappers).

title: Self-service model intake: signed-weight manifest, conversion sandbox, and deterministic smoke suite
preview: Build a self-service, end-to-end intake pipeline for community/OSS model weights that enforces signed manifests, performs reproducible conversion in an isolated sandbox, and runs a deterministic smoke test + lightweight perf profiling to decide an initial pin recommendation. This ticket covers the gateway contract a...
text: Build a self-service, end-to-end intake pipeline for community/OSS model weights that enforces signed manifests, performs reproducible conversion in an isolated sandbox, and runs a deterministic smoke test + lightweight perf profiling to decide an initial pin recommendation. This ticket covers the gateway contract and the first-phase runner (conversion + smoke) for MLP/Transformer artifacts. The goal is to reduce manual triage, shorten time-to-publish, and eliminate common conversion failure modes that block downstream runtime onboarding.

title: Demand heatmap + feasibility snapshot for Sales/CS enterprise asks
preview: Create a compact, repeatable intake artifact that Sales/CS can use to consolidate enterprise feature requests into a demand heatmap and a one-page feasibility snapshot. The goal is to reduce lead time from first-sales-ask to an actionable PM/Eng scoping decision from weeks to days. This ticket delivers: 1) an intake...
text: Create a compact, repeatable intake artifact that Sales/CS can use to consolidate enterprise feature requests into a demand heatmap and a one-page feasibility snapshot. The goal is to reduce lead time from first-sales-ask to an actionable PM/Eng scoping decision from weeks to days. This ticket delivers: 1) an intake template for frontline reps that balances signal capture with low friction, 2) an automated scoring rubric (ARR, #accounts, strategic fit, legal/regulatory risk, infra/runtime compatibility), 3) a template "feasibility snapshot" that includes rough effort band, risks, suggested model/runtime path (hosted / dedicated / private), and 4) a simple dashboard CSV schema for aggregation and heatmap generation.

title: Isolated VPC: Minimal Network Binding, Offline Upgrade Safety Contract, and Air‑gapped Acceptance Tests
preview: Purpose: Define a minimally permissive networking and offline-upgrade acceptance contract for Private/VPC deployments that must be enforced by the operator and validated by Redwood before GA. This ticket captures the requirements, objective test cases, and operational runbooks needed to certify a customer environmen...
text: Purpose: Define a minimally permissive networking and offline-upgrade acceptance contract for Private/VPC deployments that must be enforced by the operator and validated by Redwood before GA. This ticket captures the requirements, objective test cases, and operational runbooks needed to certify a customer environment as 'Offline‑Safe' (able to receive upgrades from an approved mirror) and 'Zero‑Egress Compliant' (no network connections to Redwood control plane required in normal operation). This PRD focuses on networking bindings, artifact sync/verification, upgrade choreography, observable checks, and failure-mode acceptance criteria.

title: Semantic roles & focus management sweep for components: buttons, tables, charts, tokens
preview: Goal: Complete a cross-component accessibility sweep focused on semantic roles, keyboard focus/order, skip/fallback behaviors, and contrast tokens for buttons, table controls, and data-visualization components so the design system is ready for the upcoming VPAT refresh and runtime consumers. This ticket covers desig...
text: Goal: Complete a cross-component accessibility sweep focused on semantic roles, keyboard focus/order, skip/fallback behaviors, and contrast tokens for buttons, table controls, and data-visualization components so the design system is ready for the upcoming VPAT refresh and runtime consumers. This ticket covers design decisions, token adjustments, keyboard nav matrices, ARIA role recommendations, and a rollout plan with dev/QA acceptance criteria. Scope intentionally excludes content authoring flows (rich text editor) which are covered in another ticket.

title: Automated jurisdiction-aware access recertification and SCIM drift remediation
preview: Goal: Build an automated workflow to run periodic access recertifications that are aware of tenant data residency constraints and to remediate SCIM provisioning drift for scoped service accounts. The feature should produce cryptographically verifiable evidence bundles suitable for audit exports and support per-tenan...
text:
Goal: Build an automated workflow to run periodic access recertifications that are aware of tenant data residency constraints and to remediate SCIM provisioning drift for scoped service accounts. The feature should produce cryptographically verifiable evidence bundles suitable for audit exports and support per-tenant retention policies.

Background: Reducing manual work in enterprise audits remains high priority for enterprise customers operating across jurisdictions (EU, UK, CA, US). Current periodic access exports are manual and global; they do not filter by residency zone or automatically reconcile SCIM drift discovered during recertification. This ticket ties together three capabilities: (1) jurisdiction filters applied to access recertification runs, (2) automated SCIM reconciliation suggestions + optional self-heal for service accounts, (3) evidence bundling (signed manifests) w...
